Title: Melaka snake incident: Man forces reptile into car at Stadthuys tourist spot
Article:
MELAKA: A young woman says she was left traumatised after a man allegedly forced a snake into her car near the Stadthuys in Bandar Hilir on Monday night, in an incident that has since gone viral on social media.
Nik Hanis Allysa Abdullah, 21, said the incident occurred around 11pm when she, her younger sibling and a friend had stopped near the historic landmark before being approached by a man carrying a snake.
The sales assistant said she initially thought it was a prank, but the situation escalated when the man allegedly pushed the reptile towards the inside of her vehicle through an open front window.
Although she is not afraid of snakes, she said the act was dangerous and inappropriate, posing serious safety risks.
"At that time I had just finished work and was tired. I did not expect to face such a situation.
"I repeatedly asked him not to put the snake into the car, but he continued pushing it towards me until I cried," she said.
Her 18-year-old friend, who was recording a video of a nearby street mascot, was also startled and immediately got out of the vehicle.
Nik Hanis said she panicked and exited the car while trying to avoid the snake, forgetting that the vehicle was still in drive mode.
"The car moved forward while one of my legs was still outside. Fortunately, nothing worse happened," she said.
She urged the authorities to take appropriate action against the individual, saying his actions not only frightened members of the public but could also have caused a serious accident.
Nik Hanis also uploaded a 54-second video of the incident, showing her repeatedly asking the man to stop.
She said she shared the footage to remind the public to remain cautious when visiting the popular tourist area.
Melaka Tengah police chief Superintendent Halim Abas confirmed that no police report had been lodged regarding the incident.
Meanwhile, Historic Melaka City Council mayor Datuk Shadan Othman said the council would investigate the matter.
The incident has sparked widespread outrage online, with many social media users condemning the man's actions. He is believed to have been seen previously in the area carrying a snake.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara imposes RM520,000 fine on Aeon Credit for sanctions breaches
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia has fined Aeon Credit Service (M) Bhd RM520,000 for failure to comply with targeted financial sanctions.
In a statement, the central bank said the fine was imposed on April 6 after breaches identified by the bank following an on-site supervisory examination on Aeon Credit, which revealed that a specified entity listed under the Domestic List was onboarded as its customer.
"Aeon Credit failed to reject the potential match despite it being a positive match.
"Additionally, there was also a delay in the freezing of the customer's account upon confirmation that the customer was a specified entity. These breaches were attributed to lack of staff oversight and a gap in Aeon Credit's standard operating procedure (SOP)," it said.
As a reporting institution, the central bank said that Aeon Credit is required to reject a potential customer when there is a positive match against individuals and entities listed in the Domestic List and the United Nations Security Council Resolutions List.
The Domestic List is a list of names and particulars of specified entities declared by the Home Affairs Ministry under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001.
It added that Aeon Credit has taken remedial measures by enhancing its SOP and conducting refresher training for staff to ensure compliance with targeted financial sanctions requirements.
Aeon Credit has paid the required amount on April 16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: H5 bird flu detected in second Australia state
Article:
SYDNEY: Scientists have detected the highly contagious H5 bird flu strain in a second Australian state, the nation's agriculture minister said on Wednesday.
Australia was for years the only continental landmass to be free of the H5 strain, which has caused severe disease and high death rates in poultry and wild birds worldwide.
Two H5 cases have been confirmed in migratory birds found in the state of Western Australia, with officials now warning of a third infection found hundreds of kilometres (miles) away in South Australia.
"This is obviously concerning, but given the spread of H5 globally it is not unexpected that other migratory birds may have arrived at other locations across the Australian coastline," Agriculture Minister Julie Collins told reporters.
"At this stage there is no evidence of mass mortalities. There is also no evidence of infection in poultry or in our agricultural production system," Collins added.
"There continues to be a low risk to human health."
The confirmation of H5 spread has the potential to cause significant disruptions.
One of the country's largest poultry producers has locked down farms in Western Australia in a bid to stay disease-free.
Neighbouring Papua New Guinea briefly suspended imports of Australian eggs and chicken meat – although this has since been lifted with some exceptions.
"We have continued to engage constructively with the Papua New Guinea government since their notification of suspension to have these trade restrictions removed," Collins said.
Chief government veterinary officer Beth Cookson said scientists were still piecing together the details of how H5 might be spreading.
- VULNERABLE SPECIES -
So far it seemed to be limited to migratory seabirds without threatening other vulnerable native populations, she said.
"We've only just received the advice on the South Australian detection. We're still in the investigation phase," she told reporters.
"It is a seabird, a migratory species, and there is no indication that it has spread beyond those populations."
There has been concern that the deadly disease could add to the extinction risks faced by Australian fauna, many of which are unique to the vast continent.
Almost half of Australia's wild bird species, and 83 percent of its mammals, are found nowhere else.
The wild birds most affected by the H5 strain include waterfowl, shorebirds, seabirds and birds of prey.
Marine mammals have also been affected, with some detections in other animals such as cats, goats, alpacas and pigs.
Officials have said they are investigating if the disease arrived in Australia via birds migrating from the sub-Antarctic.
Scientists said last week the H5 bird flu strain had killed more than 13,000 elephant seal pups after infecting a breeding colony on the remote Heard and McDonald Islands, one of Australia's external territories in the sub-Antarctic.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two policemen badly hurt in attack by violent drug addict
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Two policemen from the Selama police headquarters' Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) were seriously injured following a knife attack by a suspected drug addict in Kampung Sungai Jernih, Ijok, yesterday.
The duo — a constable and a sergeant — were attacked about 12pm while checking on a report about possible drug abuse at a hut in the village.
Sources said the constable suffered serious facial injuries while the sergeant sustained wounds to his head, arm and abdomen.
One source said the suspect, in his 30s, had tried to flee when he noticed the presence of policemen near his hut.
The constable managed to catch up to the suspect but the man whipped out a knife and attacked him.
The constable suffered a deep slash wound to the face extending from the forehead to the cheek, as well as injuries to his right arm.
The sergeant, who tried to assist his colleague, was also slashed and suffered injuries to his head, arm and upper abdomen.
After attacking the two policemen, the suspect fled into a nearby forest.
Both victims later sought treatment at the emergency unit of Selama Hospital.
Following the incident, police launched an operation to track down the suspect.
The suspect remains at large and investigations are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: World Cup gambling crackdown nets 1,156 arrests
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested 1,156 people and carried out 868 raids in a city-wide crackdown on illegal gambling activities linked to the FIFA World Cup 2026.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the enforcement drive, conducted under Op Soga IV, was aimed at curbing a rise in illegal betting and gambling activities during the global football tournament.
He said the operation, conducted between Jan 1 and June 23, targeted football betting syndicates, online gambling operators, illegal lottery activities, gambling dens, outdoor gambling activities and illegal calling centres linked to gambling operations.
"Among the major successes recorded were 22 raids on football betting activities, resulting in the arrest of 24 suspects and the seizure of RM25,020 in cash.
"Police also confiscated laptops, mobile phones, routers, SIM cards and other equipment believed to have been used in illegal betting operations," he said in a statement today.
Fadil said police also carried out 322 raids on online gambling operations, leading to 341 arrests, while 486 raids on illegal lottery activities resulted in 487 arrests.
"In separate operations, 16 raids on gambling dens saw 146 people detained, while one outdoor gambling raid led to the arrest of six individuals.
"Police also raided 21 premises suspected of operating as illegal calling centres, arresting 152 people and seizing computers, laptops, mobile phones, modems and routers," he said.
Overall, police seized RM45,033 in cash, while the value of football bets uncovered amounted to RM96,878.58 and HK$32,799.75.
Fadil said enforcement efforts would continue to ensure public safety, uphold the law and prevent criminal activities linked to gambling syndicates.
Anyone with information on gambling activities is urged to contact the Kuala Lumpur police hotline at 03-2115 9999.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Settled is settled, Sanusi tells Bersatu over Wawasan admission into PN
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Perikatan Nasional (PN) leader has dismissed Bersatu's objections to the admission of Parti Cinta Malaysia (PCM), which is being rebranded as Parti Wawasan Negara under Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.
PN election director Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor told Bersatu that the matter had already been settled at the coalition's meeting and should not be revisited afterwards.
Asked to comment on Bersatu's statement issued yesterday objecting to PCM's admission, Sanusi said the issue had been resolved during the meeting and that raising objections after a decision had been made was not in line with the coalition's practice.
"It is already settled. I said the day before yesterday that it was already settled and the meeting concluded well.
"Any objection after that, that's not our style. Settle it in the meeting, then it's settled," he said.
Sanusi, who is also the Kedah menteri besar, likened the coalition's decision-making process to syura, the Islamic principle of consultation. He said the binding nature of decisions reached through consultation meant component parties were expected to abide by them once a matter had been settled.
"If it was already discussed at the meeting, intentions already voiced and a decision already made, then comply.
"So if you don't comply with the meeting's decision, who are you going to comply with? Your own desires? Better to just sit alone," he said.
Sanusi also said not every proposal raised at a meeting would necessarily be accepted by all parties.
"That's what a meeting is for. If all our agenda items were agreed to, there would be no need to hold a meeting," he said.
He added that the rejection of a proposal should not be a cause for resentment, suggesting there could be wisdom behind why certain ideas were not accepted.
"You never know, if people had accepted our proposal, there might have been some harm in it. So even that itself is a blessing in our work," he said.
Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu yesterday had said that Bersatu representatives
objected
to PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar's insistence that PCM be admitted into PN.
He said the objection was due to PN facing internal issues among its component parties following Pas's decision to sever ties with Bersatu.
The statement came a day after PCM and Pejuang were admitted as new component parties of PN during Monday's supreme council meeting attended by leaders from Pas, Bersatu, Gerakan and MIPP.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former IT engineer fined RM18,000 for abusing Budi95 subsidy
Article:
BESUT: A former information technology engineer was fined RM18,000, in default 18 months' jail, by the magistrate's court today after pleading guilty to three counts of using another person's identity card.
Aman Rasid, 39, entered the plea after the charges were read before magistrate Nurliyana Md Zukri.
For the first charge, he was accused of using another person's MyKad at a petrol station in Alor Lintang at 11.33am on June 1.
He was charged with committing the same offence at a petrol station in Kampung Raja at 7.25pm on June 2 and 6.07pm on June 15 respectively.
The offences were framed under Regulation 25(1)(e) of the National Registration Regulations 1990, which carries penalties under the same regulation.
Nurliyana imposed a fine of RM6,000, in default six months' jail, for each charge.
Deputy public prosecutor Nurul Najah Azhar prosecuted while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer Nur Fazilah Abd Rahim.
According to the facts of the case, the complainant, who was the legitimate owner of the identity card discovered his monthly Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) subsidy quota had been utilised without authorisation from the Touch 'n Go (TnG) eWallet application.
The complainant, however did not have a TnG eWallet account and subsequently lodged a police report and claimed that his identity card had been used by an unknown individual to purchase fuel without his knowledge.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tempers flare in Dewan Rakyat over Kelantan convoy, illegal racing link
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tempers flared in the Dewan Rakyat today after a government member of parliament linked a motorcycle convoy in Kelantan to the rise of illegal street racing among youths.
Khoo Poay Tiong (PH–Kota Melaka) made the remarks while debating the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026, which addresses offences related to illegal racing and speed testing.
He described a motorcycle convoy organised by Melor assemblyman Wan Rohimi Wan Daud as unacceptable, claiming that such activities could encourage illegal racing behaviour among young people.
"They will go racing because they are being supported by the Kelantan government itself. We must strongly condemn the actions of the assemblyman.
"As leaders, we should set a good example, not organise motorcycle convoys to the airport during a busy period when many people were heading for haj.
"People were going to board flights in Kota Baru, but they organised a large motorcycle convoy to accompany them.
"As politicians and elected representatives, we should be setting a good example, not organising large motorcycle convoys. That is not right," he said.
The remarks by the DAP lawmaker prompted Salamiah Mohd Nor (PH–Temerloh) to intervene, clarifying that the convoy had received approval from the police.
"It was under control, not like illegal racing. I just want to clarify that it was escorted by police and had police approval. It was a convoy, not street racing," she said.
Datuk Azman Nasrudin (PN–Padang Serai), who later took over the debate, also questioned Khoo's remarks, saying he had too readily linked the convoy to the promotion of illegal racing.
In response, Khoo reiterated his view, saying such convoys could encourage repeated activities.
"The problem is, when you do it once, they will continue doing it," he said.
The
convoy
and gathering titled "Konvoi dan Lepak Anak Muda Bersama YB-YB", organised by the Backbenchers Club (BBC) and Kelantan PAS Youth in April, had previously drawn criticism on social media.
The Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026, currently being debated in Parliament, proposes penalties of up to five years' imprisonment for those convicted of illegal racing or speed testing on public roads.
Transport Minister
Anthony Loke
, in tabling the bill for second reading yesterday, said the new Section 42A under the amendment makes racing or speed testing a specific offence under the Road Transport Act 1987.
First-time offenders may face fines between RM2,000 and RM10,000, imprisonment of up to two years, or both.
Repeat offenders face higher penalties, including fines of between RM5,000 and RM20,000, imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Motorcyclist found dead in drain near Karangkraf office
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A factory worker was found dead after the motorcycle he was riding skidded and plunged into a drain along Persiaran Selangor near the Karangkraf office in Section 15 here.
Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Sarudin Samah said the victim, a man in his 40s, was found together with his motorcycle around 11.40pm yesterday.
Initial investigations revealed that the victim, who worked at a factory in Bukit Raja, was on his way home to Section 25 after finishing work when the accident is believed to have occurred around 5.40am on the same day.
"Upon reaching the scene, the motorcycle is believed to have lost control, veered to the left side of the road and fallen into a drain.
"The rider sustained severe injuries and died at the scene," he said in a statement today.
Sarudin said a post-mortem conducted at Shah Alam Hospital at 9am today determined the cause of death as chest injuries sustained in a road crash.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Police have urged anyone with information regarding the accident to contact investigating officer Inspector Zaharizul Zamri of the Shah Alam Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division at 019-3229097 to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AI could cut retail operating costs by up to 30pct: Mydin director
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Artificial intelligence (AI) is a powerful tool for retailers seeking to manage rising operating costs, with potential savings of up to 30 per cent in certain processes, Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd director Malik Murad Ali said.
Retailers are facing mounting cost pressures, particularly from labour and supply chain expenses, as transportation and operating costs continue to rise.
To mitigate these challenges, Malik said AI is helping businesses optimise operations, improve productivity and preserve profit margins without having to pass on additional costs to consumers.
"Depending on the process, savings can reach 20-30 per cent. In some cases, the savings may only be two per cent, but at a large scale, even a two per cent improvement can translate into RM20 million," he said.
He said this to reporters after a roundtable "AI Impact on Retail Margins & Cost Pressures" here today.
Malik added that AI can enhance efficiency across multiple areas of a retail operation including logistics, route planning and workforce management.
For instance, AI-driven data analysis can identify shorter and more efficient delivery routes, helping retailers reduce fuel consumption and transportation costs.
He also highlighted the technology's role in staff training, where AI-powered systems have significantly shortened learning curves for new employees.
Tasks that previously required one to two days of training can now be mastered within an hour, enabling retailers to onboard workers more quickly and expand employment opportunities for students, retirees and single mothers seeking part-time work.
Beyond workforce management, retailers are increasingly using AI alongside automation and energy-efficiency initiatives to lower operational expenses.
Malik said AI can be used to optimise the operation of air-conditioning systems, particularly in large facilities such as distribution centres and cold-chain operations, where electricity consumption is substantial.
He said such optimisation efforts can generate daily savings of between RM10,000 and RM20,000, while helping businesses better manage utility costs.
In addition, retailers are investing in LED lighting, solar energy and backend automation to improve operational efficiency and maintain pricing competitiveness.
"Retailers do not want to raise prices. What we need to do is optimise our backend operations and reduce costs wherever possible. When costs come down, it becomes easier to maintain margins and keep prices affordable for customers," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MyMall, TikTok Shop drive digital growth among MSMEs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is continuing to strengthen the country's digital entrepreneurship ecosystem through various initiatives aimed at enhancing the competitiveness, productivity and market access of local entrepreneurs, both domestically and internationally.
Deputy Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Datuk Mohamad Alamin said the efforts are being implemented under the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) Strategic Plan 2030, which focuses on digital transformation and sustainable business development.
He said the approach is intended to expand opportunities for local entrepreneurs in the growing digital economy, including through online marketing platforms and capacity-building programmes.
"It is aimed at transforming MSMEs in Malaysia into more sustainable businesses that are competitive and able to adapt to emerging trends and unforeseen challenges," he said in the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to a question from Datuk Mohd Kurniawan Naim Moktar (BN-Kinabatangan) on the government's efforts to enhance the capabilities of local digital entrepreneurs.
Mohamad said one of the key initiatives is the MyMall platform, which serves as a free e-commerce marketplace for local MSMEs and cooperatives.
"Since its launch in 2022, a total of 5,776 entrepreneurs have registered on the platform, generating sales of RM24.5 million," he said.
He added that strategic cooperation with TikTok Shop through Tekun Nasional has also been expanded, including the provision of live-streaming studios.
"So far, 1,054 entrepreneurs have utilised the platform, generating total sales of RM35.1 million.
"Training and mentoring programmes under the National Entrepreneurship Institute have also been strengthened to enhance digital entrepreneurship skills."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No proof of electrocution in UUM student's death, court rules
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Utara Malaysia (UUM) was not liable for the death of student Vinosiny R. Sivakumar, who was found dead in a dormitory room in 2022, the High Court has ruled.
Judge Dr Johan Lee made the ruling when dismissing a RM3.05 million lawsuit against the university filed by Vinosiny's father, R. Sivakumar Rajoo, recently.
The judge held that the plaintiff failed to prove, on a balance of probabilities, that Vinosiny died from electrocution or that the university had breached its duty of care.
The court said the plaintiff's case was not supported by objective technical evidence, adding that independent investigations established the safety of the university's electrical installations.
Vinosiny, 21, was found lifeless on the floor of her room at a student residential hall on May 21, 2022.
Following her death, Sivakumar filed the suit against UUM on Feb 28, 2024, alleging that the university's negligence and failure to properly maintain the dormitory's electrical installations caused his daughter's death.
He sought RM50,000 in special damages and RM1 million each in general, aggravated and exemplary damages.
The plaintiff's case relied heavily on a post-mortem report, which concluded that electrocution was the cause of death.
However, Johan, in his grounds of judgment, said the medical evidence could not be safely relied upon.
He said the forensic medical officer who prepared the report had limited experience handling electrocution cases and did not conduct definitive tests capable of scientifically establishing the entry of electric current into the body.
He added that the medical conclusion was contradicted by the findings of the Energy Commission, which conducted independent technical investigations into the incident.
The commission found no evidence of electrical leakage in the room and concluded that the room's electrical installations complied with statutory safety requirements.
Johan said tests conducted on the room's bed, study table, chair and metal fixtures showed no electrical leakage, while electrical items seized from the room were functioning properly.
"Premised on the objective technical findings of the Energy Commission, this court finds as a fact that the defendant's electrical systems were safe, compliant, and free from any leakage," he said.
The judge also rejected allegations that a defective wall lamp caused the fatal incident.
He described the claim that a university staff member had repaired the wall lamp before the incident and left it in a dangerous condition as a "legally baseless and uncorroborated afterthought".
"Therefore, this court finds that the plaintiff's assertion that the defendant possessed actual or constructive knowledge of the alleged defect is completely unsubstantiated," he said.
The court noted that Vinosiny suffered from myocardial fibrosis, a pre-existing heart condition which lowered the threshold for fatal cardiac arrhythmia.
Given the absence of proof of electrocution, the court held that a sudden lethal arrhythmia linked to the condition remained a plausible alternative cause of death.
"Therefore, this court rejects the conclusion of the postmortem report and finds that the plaintiff has failed to establish, on a balance of probabilities, that electrocution was the operative cause of the deceased's death," he said.
On the issue of negligence, the judge ruled that the plaintiff failed to establish any breach of duty by UUM.
The court also rejected the plaintiff's reliance on the doctrine of res ipsa loquitur, ruling that it could not be invoked where specific acts of negligence had been pleaded but not proven.
Lawyers M. Manoharan and M. Hariharan appeared for the plaintiff while Barvina Ponnusamy and Sathisharajah Yuvarajah represented the defendant.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No new subsidy burden under Budi Madani Diesel: Liew Chin Tong
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The streamlined Budi Madani Diesel mechanism will not expand subsidy programmes or increase government subsidy expenditure, ensuring fiscal discipline is maintained.
Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the initiative instead consolidates existing assistance into a more efficient and targeted support system.
He said all diesel subsidies are now placed under the Budi Madani framework, including assistance that was previously channelled through other methods to eligible recipients.
He added that the measure will enable the government to reduce subsidy leakages, particularly those arising from smuggling activities and the use of subsidised fuel by foreigners.
"There is no expansion of the subsidy programme. Essentially, we are now placing all subsidies, including diesel, under the Budi Madani framework. Previously, diesel users received cash subsidies and additional assistance separately.
"Now, everything is streamlined through the Budi Madani system to ensure a more organised and effective delivery of assistance," he said during a firechat session at the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Mid-Year Tax Focus Conference 2026 here today.
Liew also noted the technology used under the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) system has proven effective, allowing the government to ensure that subsidies are enjoyed only by eligible Malaysians while closing loopholes for abuse by irresponsible parties.
The Budi Madani framework ensures smugglers can no longer purchase subsidised fuel, while foreigners are also prevented from benefiting from subsidised rates.
"This will reduce the government's financial burden by curbing smuggling activities and preventing foreigners from obtaining subsidised petrol or diesel," he said.
The Finance Ministry previously announced that 700,000 private diesel vehicle owners will be eligible to buysubsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre using their MyKad under the Budi Diesel mechanism at all petrol stations.
In addition, the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) has been expanded to Sabah and Sarawak, enabling about 70,000 eligible commercial vehicles, particularly those involved in transporting consumer goods, to enjoy subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre through oil company fleet cards.
Under the mechanism, each eligible individual will receive a basic monthly entitlement of up to 200 litres.
This quota can be used flexibly according to recipients' actual needs for the purchase of subsidised diesel or subsidised RON95, subject to their remaining monthly entitlement.
The approach ensures that subsidies are provided to eligible recipients based on their actual fuel consumption, regardless of whether they use diesel or RON95.
It also gives recipients greater flexibility to manage their fuel usage according to their monthly requirements.
Based on available statistics, nearly 90 per cent of drivers in Malaysia use less than 200 litres of fuel per month.
As such, the 200-litre basic entitlement is expected to be sufficient for most eligible recipients.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Che'gu Bard ordered to enter defence over defamation against King
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court today reversed the acquittal of political activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin and ordered him to enter his defence on a charge of defaming His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, the King of Malaysia, over statements concerning a purported audience with political leaders two years ago.
Judge Noor Ruwena Md Nurdin made the ruling after allowing the prosecution's appeal against the Sessions Court's decision last November, which had
acquitted
the 47-year-old at the end of the prosecution's case.
"Having heard the parties' submissions and examined the record of appeal, the court is satisfied that there is credible evidence showing that the statement made by the respondent was untrue and amounted to defamation.
"Therefore, the prosecution has succeeded in establishing a prima facie case against the respondent (Badrul Hisham) and, as such, he is called upon to enter his defence on the charge.
"Accordingly, the court orders that the case be remitted to the Sessions Court before the same judge for the defence stage to proceed," she said.
Badrul Hisham, the former Port Dickson Bersatu division chief, better known as Che'gu Bard, who was present in court, appeared calm upon hearing the decision.
On Nov 4 last year, Sessions Court judge Norma Ismail had acquitted him after ruling that the prosecution failed to establish a prima facie case.
The Attorney-General's Chambers filed an appeal on the same day.
In her decision today, Ruwena said that at the end of the prosecution's case, the explanation that remained unrefuted was the alleged audience with the King.
"In reality, it did not happen. If it had occurred as claimed, the palace would surely have withdrawn the police report. That was never done.
"According to the palace, there was no consent from the King for the meeting.
"This means that no such meeting was consented to and, in fact, it did not take place.
"Even by the close of the prosecution's case, there was no evidence or statement confirming that the meeting had occurred," she said.
She said there was no finding that the palace's media liaison officer, Mohd Husni Yusop, who was instructed by the comptroller of the Istana Negara Royal Household to lodge a police report over the matter, was not a credible witness or that his testimony should not be believed.
"SP1 (Husni) explained that there was no consent given and the meeting did not happen. So that amounted to defamation and forms the crux of the prosecution's case," she said.
Ruwena ordered the case to be mentioned at the Sessions Court tomorrow, with a trial date to be fixed.
The prosecution was represented by deputy public prosecutor Faizal @ Amrin Noor Hadi, while lawyer Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali acted for Badrul Hisham.
In April last year, Badrul Hisham was charged with making defamatory statements where he had reason to believe they would damage the reputation of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.
The alleged offence took place at 6pm at Istana Negara on Jan 22.
The charge under Section 500 of the Penal Code provides for a maximum jail term of two years, a fine, or both upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Excitement grows as BN leaders, supporters gather for candidate announcements
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor Umno headquarters is all abuzz as Barisan Nasional leaders, members and supporters have begun gathering for the much-awaited announcement of candidates for the 16th state election.
While the announcement was set for 2.30pm, people and the media began gathering there as early as noon.
The announcement was later postponed to 4.30pm.
It is understood the postponement is because there are several seats in which the candidates have not been finalised.
Among those seen were Umno treasurer Datuk Seri Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansor and Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba.
Also present was Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh, who held the Bukit Pasir state seat before the legislative assembly was dissolved on June 1.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Haim Hilman to appear in court over university admission claims
Article:
ALOR SETAR: Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah will be charged at the Seremban magistrate's court tomorrow over allegations related to claims he previously made concerning access to higher education.
Haim Hilman, who is also Kedah's Industry and Investment, Higher Education, Science, Technology and Innovation committee chairman, said he would appear in court at 9am to face the charge.
In a statement, he said he was prepared to give his full cooperation throughout the legal process.
Previously, Haim Hilman was reported as saying he was ready to face legal action over his claims regarding the alleged admission of students into public universities without going through the University Central Unit (UPU) channel.
The media had also reported that the Higher Education Ministry viewed seriously several allegations concerning the public university admission system that had been repeatedly raised by Haim Hilman and was considering legal action over the matter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: France heatwave triggers major blackout, 68,000 homes hit
Article:
RENNES, France: Europe's record-breaking heatwave left around 68,000 households without electricity in northwestern France on Wednesday, authorities said, in the country's first major power outage of the latest bout of extreme weather.
The outage, which involved a transformer on the electricity grid, was related to extreme temperatures and did not injure anyone, the prefecture in the coastal department of Finistere said in a statement.
The incident took place around 9pm (1900 GMT) on Tuesday in the commune of Ergue-Gaberic near the city of Quimper in Brittany, the prefecture added.
While teams from French grid operators RTE and Enedis had worked through the night to fix the issue, power is not expected to be restored in full until the end of the day at the earliest.
Up to 106,000 clients of the French power network were left without power by late Tuesday, as the continent's sweltering heatwave pushed the country into its hottest day ever.
"For technical reasons, RTE will not be able to re-connect the affected households during the course of the day; connections will be made, at the earliest, by the end of Wednesday," the operator said.
Finistere is one of 58 French departments under the highest red alert for extreme heat on Wednesday, with temperatures of 39C to 41C expected on Wednesday from Brittany to the Paris region.
The extreme weather is being driven by atmospheric and circulation patterns that keep hot air trapped in place for days, with these factors worsened by global warming, experts say.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thailand's 'Michael Jackson' jailed over deadly drink-driving crash
Article:
BANGKOK: A Thai court jailed on Wednesday a singer dubbed Thailand's Michael Jackson for two years for killing two people while driving while drunk.
Manasawin Nantasen, known as Tik Shiro, rose to fame in the late 1980s and 1990s for his signature fedora hat, leather jackets and slick dance moves, earning comparisons to global pop icon Michael Jackson.
The 64-year-old singer was driving his vehicle across a bridge in Bangkok in October 2024 when he hit a stationary motorbike.
He killed a 28-year-old woman who was on the bike, and knocked her brother, 21, off the bridge, leaving him critically injured, police said.
The brother later died in hospital from his injuries.
A video of the singer kneeling in grief at the scene went viral across Thailand, drawing attention in a country where influential figures are sometimes perceived as enjoying impunity.
A criminal court in Bangkok on Wednesday sentenced Manasawin to two years in prison for "drunk driving resulting in loss of life."
"The defendant surrendered and provided testimony beneficial to the case," the court statement said.
It added that Manasawin had shown remorse by attending the victims' funeral and paying compensation to their families, but ordered the revocation of his driver's license.
In another case involving a celebrity, rock musician Sek Loso was released from prison on Wednesday after serving just over a year for drugs and illegal firearm possession.
However, many Thais believe that the rich and famous are not always held accountable.
In 2012, the heir to the Red Bull fortune, Vorayuth Yoovidhya, was accused of ploughing his Ferrari into a policeman in a wealthy Bangkok neighbourhood, killing him on the spot.
There was an investigation but he was never charged.
A son of former Pheu Thai MP Chalerm Yubamrung allegedly shot a man dead in a crowded Bangkok nightclub after a dispute in 2001, but was later acquitted on grounds of weak evidence. --AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Two injured after motorcycle crashes into car in Kuala Selangor
Article:
KUALA SELANGOR: Police are investigating a road accident that left a motorcyclist and his pillion rider injured after their motorcycle crashed into the rear of a car along Jalan Kuala Selangor-Kuala Lumpur on Sunday.
Kuala Selangor police chief Superintendent Azaharudin Tajudin said the district traffic complaints counter received a report on the incident at 8.36pm involving a Proton Iriz and a Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycle.
Initial investigations found that the Proton Iriz, driven by a woman, was travelling in the left lane from Kuala Lumpur towards Kuala Selangor when it was struck from behind by the motorcycle.
The motorcycle was ridden by a man, with a woman riding pillion.
Azaharudin said both the rider and pillion passenger sustained injuries and were taken to Tanjong Karang Hospital for treatment.
He said investigations revealed that the crash occurred following a misunderstanding between the Yamaha rider and another motorcyclist riding a similar motorcycle while travelling towards Kuala Selangor.
"The actions of the other motorcyclist caused the victim's motorcycle to lose control before crashing into the rear of the car in front," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.
Police have urged anyone with information on the incident to contact investigating officer Sergeant Major Shamsudin Wahab at 012-6102473 or the Kuala Selangor district police headquarters operations room at 03-32891222.
The statement was issued following the circulation of a viral video on social media showing a group of motorcyclists riding together.
The footage showed several Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycles travelling at high speed before one of the riders appeared to lose control amid close interactions between the motorcycles.d
Police also reminded all road users to comply with traffic regulations and prioritise safety on the road.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 14 Malaysians receive Yang di-Pertuan Agong Scholarship 2026
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today presented the prestigious Yang di-Pertuan Agong Scholarship (BYDPA) 2026 to 14 outstanding Malaysians at Istana Negara.
The recipients comprise seven doctoral (PhD) candidates and seven master's by research students, reflecting the government's continued emphasis on developing high-impact talent for the nation's strategic sectors.
Of the total, four scholars will pursue their studies at leading universities abroad, while the remaining 10 will further their research at local research universities.
In a statement, the Public Service Department (PSD) described the BYDPA as the country's most prestigious scholarship programme, introduced in 2006 to nurture high-achieving Malaysians with strong academic records and active involvement in co-curricular, community and innovation-based activities.
Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz said the government, through the PSD, would continue strengthening talent development initiatives aligned with national strategic priorities and public sector needs.
He said sustained investment in human capital development through scholarship programmes was essential to producing world-class expertise in strategic fields and enhancing national productivity and competitiveness.
The BYDPA covers priority disciplines, including Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (Stem), economics, Islamic finance, law and artificial intelligence, in line with efforts to strengthen expertise in high-impact sectors.
Since its introduction, 239 Malaysians have benefited from the scholarship, comprising 124 PhD recipients and 115 master's by research scholars.
The government also expressed appreciation to Sultan Ibrahim for consenting to grace the ceremony and congratulated the recipients, urging them to make full use of the opportunity to contribute to national development.
He said 13 recipients attended the ceremony, while the remaining recipient, Waywen Loh Chang Yuan, was unable to be present as he had already begun his studies at the University of Oxford in the United Kingdom (UK).
"We hope that these sponsored students, upon their return, will serve the country in their respective fields, mainly in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (Stem), as well as artificial intelligence (AI).
"We will continue to monitor their progress to ensure that their studies are completed within the stipulated period," he said.
The PhD scholarship recipients are Afiefah Zulkifli, who is pursuing studies in Nutrition at Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM); Aiman Zakwan Azman (Pharmacology and Pharmacy, Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM)); and Chua Hui Qi (Mathematics and Statistics, UPM).
Other recipients are Giroon Ijod (Nutrition, UPM); Haimon Franklin (Biology, UKM); Lee Yu Shi (Catalysis, Universiti Malaya); and Waywen Loh (Immunology, University of Oxford).
Those taking up Masters are Amirul Haziq Azwan (Biology, University College London, UK); Chan Ler Yie (Pharmacology and Pharmacy, University College London); Chris Ee Jia Wen (Genetics and Heredity, Universiti Sains Malaysia); Lim Fang (Biochemistry, UKM); Mohamad Izuwansah Ishak (Environment, UPM); Muhammad Zulhusni Che Razali (Environment, Imperial College London, UK); and Putri Najihah Athirah Zamrifana (Veterinary Science, UPM).
Meanwhile, Chua said the award represents a great trust and responsibility for her to study hard and serve the country.
She said her research involves the study of flood issues in Malaysia, using statistical methods and data from the Department of Irrigation and Drainage to assist the government and relevant agencies in developing flood mitigation projects.
"I aspire to become a researcher and lecturer in the future and also hope to encourage friends who wish to further their studies to apply for this scholarship," he said.
Another recipient Zulhusni, who is also the Dungun district engineer, said the BYDPA award reflects a great trust placed in him to contribute to the country's flood management efforts.
"I will do my best and carry this trust to the best of my ability for the sake of my beloved country, Malaysia, Insya-Allah," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Yinson's FSO vessel ready for Vietnam deployment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Production has reached a milestone with the naming of its floating storage and offloading (FSO) vessel FSO PTSC Lac Da Vang ahead of its deployment to the Lac Da Vang project offshore Vietnam.
The vessel was contracted by Murphy Cuu Long Bac Oil Co Ltd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Murphy Oil Corp, to provide storage and offloading services for the Lac Da Vang field in Block 15-1/05 offshore Vietnam.
"The contract has a firm period of 10 years with an option to extend for up to an additional five years," it said.
The vessel is designed as a newbuild double-hull vessel with a storage capacity of about 500,000 barrels and is expected to commence operations in the fourth quarter of 2026.
It incorporates advanced dual-fuel systems to support operational efficiency while contributing to lower emissions.
The naming ceremony follows the vessel's launch at Nantong StrongWind Shipyard in China earlier this year.
The project is now progressing through final outfitting, commissioning and testing stages before its planned sail-away and deployment to Vietnam.
Yinson Production chief executive officer Flemming Guiducci Grønnegaard said the naming marks an important milestone in the journey towards deployment and first operations at the Lac Da Vang field.
"This project reflects the strength of our partnership with PTSC and the dedication of the teams who have worked tirelessly to bring the vessel to this stage," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Private university student charged with trafficking RM1.17mil worth of cannabis buds [WATCH]
Article:
JITRA: A private university student was charged in the magistrate's court here today with trafficking 14.63kg of cannabis buds worth RM1.17 million at the Bukit Kayu Hitam Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex last week.
No plea was recorded from Ishvherr Singh Jagjit Singh, 24, after the charge was read to him before magistrate Nor Syaliati Mohd Sobri, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The offence was alleged to have been committed at a roadblock at the Bukit Kayu Hitam ICQS Complex while entering Malaysia from Thailand at 11.46am on June 15.
The charge was framed under Section 39B(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, punishable under Section 39B(2) of the same, which carries the death penalty or life imprisonment and a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan, upon conviction.
Inspector Sharifah Nurlin Atiqah Wan Idrus prosecuted, while the accused was represented by Datuk Naran Singh.
The court fixed Oct 5 for mention pending the submission of the chemistry report before the case is transferred to the High Court.
It was previously reported that the suspect, believed to have acted as a transporter, was arrested while driving a pickup truck after allegedly attempting to smuggle 14.63kg of cannabis buds worth RM1.17 million from a neighbouring country.
Deputy Kedah police chief Datuk Baderulhisham Baharudin had said a further inspection of the vehicle uncovered 13 black plastic packages containing substances believed to be cannabis hidden behind the rear seat.
He said the suspect, who is from Rawang, Selangor, also tested positive for cannabis.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ByteDance seeks US$20 billion in its largest-ever offshore loan
Article:
BEIJING: Chinese technology company ByteDance, the developer of TikTok, is in preliminary talks with banks for its largest offshore loan of about US$20 billion, Bloomberg News reported on Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.
The company has approached banks for a loan that may carry a three-year tenor, with an option to extend it to as long as five years, the report said.
ByteDance did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.
The company is emerging as a major spender on AI infrastructure, ramping up spending and partnerships to secure chips and chip design services.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former food delivery CEO rebuilds life after company collapse
Article:
KOTA BARU: From humble beginnings, Indera Aria Wee Jeeny rose to become chief executive officer (CEO) of a food delivery platform company before losing it all.
Although he did not come from a well-off family, Indera, 40, once found success as CEO of a food ordering and delivery platform company that began operations in October 2019.
At its peak, the company recorded profits of up to RM6 million and had 3,000 active food riders across Kelantan, Terengganu and Kedah.
He said the company remained a strategic partner under the Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation, but later suffered losses after an Internet Protocol (IP) address was allegedly sold by an individual to a competitor for personal gain.
From January 2021, the situation worsened, with profits falling to between RM40,000 and RM80,000 before the business was on the verge of collapse.
As the business declined, Indera was forced to sell assets to pay employee salaries.
Sharing his experience, the father of two said his life changed completely after the collapse, leaving him unable to afford food.
"It was not easy to get back up because when I first fell, I even had to dig for coins to buy food… I also had to walk long distances because I had no transport.
"At that time, I began to notice changes in people around me, especially those who were once close but started to distance themselves, possibly because they feared I would ask to borrow money.
"It was painful because when I was doing well, I had helped them," he told Harian Metro.
"But that is reality I had to accept, and after self-reflection, I learned to accept God's will."
He said there were moments when he almost gave up on rebuilding his life.
However, he regained motivation after recalling the words of a former employee, a food rider who remained grateful despite low earnings.
"At that time, he only earned RM20 but was very happy because he could buy food for his child.
"Those words gave me motivation and hope to rise again," he said.
Now, Indera works independently as a food rider and takes up jobs purchasing items for a fee of between RM5 and RM10.
He also helps people in need he meets along the roadside, especially those requiring assistance with vehicle batteries and tyres.
"I have been at the bottom and lost hope, so I do not want others to feel the same.
"So while delivering food orders, I will also look around and help those in need.
"I meet people in need almost every night, and once came across a family who had not eaten for two weeks. I am not well off either, but I try to help as much as I can.
"This is because I remember the advice to never let a day pass without helping others. It feels fulfilling," he said.
Indera said he now considers what happened in his life a test and learning experience.
"I never expected to go through all this, but I will use the experience as a guide for the future.
"I am also determined to rise again as a successful entrepreneur and create job opportunities so I can help more people out there," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH man challenges Onn Hafiz to name Felda 'blockers'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor PH communications director Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali today challenged Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi to identify those who allegedly prevented him from entering a Felda settlement.
Sheikh Umar accused the Johor BN chairman of creating a political narrative ahead of the July 11 state election.
He said the caretaker menteri besar's claim raised more questions than answers, as Onn Hafiz had yet to name the person or authority responsible for the alleged restriction.
"The most basic question is: who actually stopped him?
"Was it Felda itself, security personnel, the settlement management, or were they acting on instructions from another party?" he said in a statement today.
Yesterday, Onn Hafiz alleged that he was barred from entering a
Felda
settlement to present land titles to settlers between June 20 and 21.
However, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who oversees Felda and Felda Global Ventures (FGV), denied that any instruction had been issued to prevent Onn Hafiz from entering any Felda settlement.
"I have never
prevented
the caretaker Johor menteri besar from entering Felda areas," said Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister.
Sheikh Umar, who is also a DAP central executive committee member, said Onn Hafiz had merely made a general allegation without identifying the party responsible.
He questioned whether the claim was being used to create the perception that the menteri besar was being unfairly treated during the election campaign.
"We are now in the midst of an election campaign. The public has every right to ask whether such statements are merely a political narrative aimed at creating the perception that he is being victimised.
"If it is true that someone prevented the Johor menteri besar from entering a Felda settlement, then the matter is very serious. Onn Hafiz must come forward openly and state clearly who issued the instruction," he said.
Sheikh Umar said failure to do so would only fuel speculation that the allegation was being amplified to suggest the existence of a conspiracy against the BN leader as campaigning intensifies ahead of polling day.
The former Paloh assemblyman also took a swipe at Onn Hafiz, saying the menteri besar should stop acting like a "drama king" by making vague claims unsupported by facts.
"As menteri besar, Onn Hafiz does not need to become a drama king by making unclear allegations without presenting the full facts.
"He should stop building baseless political sympathy narratives ahead of the state election," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Can Pakistan's peacekeeping role in Iran war give it an economic dividend?
Article:
PAKISTAN'S role in brokering a peace deal in the Iran war has led to widespread diplomatic acclaim that could bring Islamabad some economic benefits, but analysts question whether such gains can help resolve the fault lines in its economy.
Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir attended talks between Iran and the US in the Swiss town of Buergenstock last weekend, the culmination of Pakistan's months-long role in one of the world's most consequential diplomatic negotiations.
"This guy. What's up, man?" US Vice President JD Vance said upon seeing Munir in the resort town before giving the army chief a hug.
Both sides, along with several world leaders, have thanked Islamabad for helping ease a conflict that could have disrupted the Strait of Hormuz for a long period, choked global oil supplies and shattered the world economy.
The breakthrough has raised Pakistan's profile and analysts say the country of 250 million people has an opportunity to convert that goodwill into some gains for an economy marked by decades of boom and bust.
But they said any benefits were unlikely to fix deeper structural issues including social and economic inequity, a narrow tax base and repeated IMF bailouts.
Pakistan is targeting economic growth of four per cent and inflation of 8.2 per cent for the coming fiscal year, compared with ​3.7 per cent projected growth in fiscal 2026 which ends in June, and 6.7 per cent average inflation in the July-May period ​of the outgoing ⁠year.
"A nation that delivers stability at home and helps advance stability abroad becomes a more credible destination for investment," said Khurram Schehzad, adviser to Pakistan's finance minister.
"A growth-oriented economic agenda, coupled with a reputation as a force for peace and stability, places Pakistan in a uniquely favourable position to attract investment into its people, infrastructure, technology and future growth sectors."
Many analysts are expecting some largesse from the US, although there have been no signs of any such windfalls yet.
Alex Vatanka, senior fellow and director of the Iran programme at the Middle East Institute in Washington, said one gain for Pakistan was the "huge potential to be a more integrated part of the broader Middle East," and eventually forging broader economic partnerships in the region that would also encompass defence.
Another possibility was that sanctions relief on Iran could allow "huge trade between Iran and Pakistan," particularly through their Balochistan land border, said Miftah Ismail, a former finance minister.
After the Sept 11, 2001 attacks and the US invasion of Afghanistan, alignment with Washington helped secure debt rescheduling from more than a dozen bilateral creditors, renewed support from the IMF and other multilateral lenders, and US assistance.
But Pakistan failed to take advantage because of structural weaknesses, analysts say.
Khurram Husain, an economic commentator and journalist, said the current situation was similar to post-9/11, but with one crucial difference: that moment came at "the start of a long ruinous war in which Pakistan had to play a frontline role" while this time "Pakistan is playing the role of a peacemaker."
[[nid:1471518]]
That distinction means Pakistan's leverage this time comes from being useful to multiple sides simultaneously — Washington, Teheran, Gulf states, Turkiye and China.
Former finance minister Ismail said the diplomatic role had enhanced Pakistan's international prestige, but that had no effect on the high costs, weak exports and external repayments that keep it dependent on the IMF.
"Our house is in such disorder that foreigners can't really help us unless we help ourselves," he said. "Nothing here in this war changes that and we will be continually dependent on the IMF."
Asim Ijaz Khwaja, a professor at Harvard University and director of the Harvard Center for International Development, said Pakistan should resist short-term financial concessions that do not raise productivity.
Instead, he said, Pakistan should seek academic exchanges and scholarships, preferential market access for textiles and IT services, technology transfer and green investment frameworks.
Hamish Falconer, Britain's minister for the Middle East, thanked Islamabad for its peacekeeping role during a visit last week and told Reuters the UK saw "huge scope for deepening trade links" with Pakistan and that a British trade minister was expected to visit in the coming months.
Diplomats from two other Western countries have also said their governments are exploring strengthening economic ties following Islamabad's peace efforts. They did not wish to be identified further.
Atif Mian, professor of economics, public policy and finance at Princeton University, said Pakistan should avoid treating diplomacy as another route to deposits, rollovers or IMF-style relief.
The real prize, he said, was a "peace pivot" — external and domestic — built on regional trade, energy links with Iran, and deeper integration with the Gulf and Turkiye through exports, technology transfer and co-dependent industries.
Analysts said any new economic gains would not fix Pakistan's deeper constraints.
"If structural reforms are not implemented, the country is poised for an implosion in coming decades," said Adeel Malik, associate professor of development economics at Oxford University.
"There are deep-seated grievances among the young and the shrinking middle classes against Pakistan's ruling elite.
The prevailing system has given ruling elites an extended lease of life but has made the country socially and economically insecure."
* The writers are from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'It used to be noises, now it shows itself'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A woman in Tumpat, Kelantan, claims her family has been experiencing unexplained disturbances at their home since late May, leaving them living in fear and unable to sleep peacefully.
Nur Aina Amalina Karim, 31, said she, her husband Mohd Faisal Usop, 31, their four children aged between seven and 13, and a 15-year-old nephew began experiencing what they described as "mystical disturbances" on May 25.
The family, who live in Kampung Kutan Sungai, said they started hearing unexplained sounds several days after Hari Raya Aidiladha.
"Before this, we never experienced anything like this. Then we began hearing various sounds, including laughter, crying, screaming and what sounded like children's voices," she said when contacted.
Nur Aina, a shop assistant, said the incidents had left her children frightened.
She claimed that the disturbances had recently escalated from sounds to alleged sightings, causing greater anxiety among the children, particularly at night.
"Previously we only heard noises, but lately it has started showing itself," she claimed.
Nur Aina said about 10 religious teachers and traditional healers had been invited to the house in an effort to stop the disturbances.
"However, after they leave, the disturbances return," she alleged.
According to her, the children have been the most affected.
She said the family sleeps on separate floors, with her and her husband occupying an upstairs bedroom while the children sleep downstairs.
"Whenever they become frightened, they scream and cry, forcing us to rush downstairs to calm them," she said.
The situation, she added, has left both her and her husband exhausted and stressed due to frequent disruptions to their sleep.
Nur Aina said she shared the family's experience on social media in the hope of finding religious teachers or healers who might be able to help.
However, she said some social media users accused the family of seeking attention.
"People can say whatever they want because they are not experiencing it themselves. If they were in our situation, perhaps they would understand what we are going through," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 3,150 teachers hired on contract to meet 2027 schooling needs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry has appointed 3,150 teachers on a Contract of Service (CoS) basis to ensure teacher readiness ahead of the implementation of a two-cohort Year One intake system in 2027, involving both 6-year-old and 7-year-old pupils.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the original target was to recruit up to 20,000 teachers if all pupils in the 6-year-old cohort — estimated at around 400,000 — were enrolled.
However, he said current registrations for the cohort stand at 73,386 pupils, resulting in a revised immediate staffing requirement.
He said the 3,150 contract appointments are intended to meet urgent staffing needs in primary schools based on current enrolment data and actual requirements.
"The special recruitment exercise does not replace the existing professional teaching career pathway, which remains the main route for teacher planning in the ministry.
"The ministry is working to ensure that reserve candidates are offered appointments as soon as possible."
Regarding reserve candidates, Wong said there are two batches of reserve applicants — from July 2025 and December 2025 — totalling 4,417 candidates.
"These candidates were not initially appointed mainly due to an oversupply in their chosen teaching subjects, resulting in delayed placements until vacancies become available.
"The largest number of cases involve Moral Education and Guidance and Counselling, although other subject areas are also affected."
As of now, 857 reserve candidates have been appointed and have reported for duty, while 20 are in the process of placement.
Wong said a further 2,633 candidates will receive appointment offers soon, in the near future.
"The remaining 907 candidates are still affected by oversupply in their chosen subjects, and the ministry is addressing this.
"For candidates whose reserve period ends in July, extensions will be granted."
Wong said the ministry is implementing various infrastructure readiness measures, including the construction of 2,596 new classrooms using the IBS modular method across 838 schools, with completion expected this year.
He added that the ministry is also carrying out upgrading and refurbishment works on existing facilities, including optimising learning spaces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Sanusi: PN settles all seat negotiations for Johor election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PN election director Datuk Seri Sanusi Md Nor today announced that seat negotiations for the Johor election among the coalition's component parties have been concluded, with 34 overlapping seats resolved.
Sanusi, who is also Kedah menteri besar, said Bersatu would contest the largest number of seats, although he declined to disclose the coalition's overall seat allocation.
"Alhamdulillah, negotiations among PN component parties have been completed. My task was to negotiate the overlapping seats.
"The seats that did not overlap were straightforward. The overlapping seats have all been resolved, alhamdulillah," he told reporters outside Pas headquarters here today.
Among those attending the meeting were PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, Johor Pas election director Tan Sri Annuar Musa, Bersatu vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu, Gerakan president Dominic Lau, and Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) president P. Punithan.
Sanusi said all candidates contesting under the negotiated arrangement would use the PN logo.
He also dismissed any suggestion of dissatisfaction among component parties over the outcome of the seat negotiations.
He said Pas would officially launch its candidate announcements at the party's operations room in Muar tomorrow night, although he was unsure whether Bersatu would hold a separate launch or join the announcement.
"It's settled — if there's no more wrestling (for seats), then it's settled," he said.
Sanusi said all component parties would campaign under the PN banner regardless of which party's candidate was contesting a particular seat, adding that the ultimate objective was to secure votes for PN as a whole in Johor.
He said the final seat allocation took into account several weighted factors, including party machinery strength, voter support, experience and incumbency.
"All of these are the weightages that determine who should be considered for a particular seat," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 110 investigation papers opened under Printing Presses Act as of June 22
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 110 investigation papers were opened under the Printing Presses and Publications Act 1984 this year up till June 22.
Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said all cases are still under investigation.
"This Act is the main legislation enacted to regulate the use of printing presses, printing, importation, production, publication and distribution of printed materials in the country.
"It is currently under review to ensure it is more progressive and sustainable, and remains relevant over time, while continuing to prioritise national security and public order," he said in a written parliamentary reply yesterday.
Saifuddin was responding to Teresa Kok (PH-Seputeh) on statistics relating to investigations, prosecutions and convictions under the Act.
She also asked whether the government would review the law to safeguard freedom of expression.
Saifuddin said the Federal Constitution does not grant absolute freedom to citizens but instead provides that such rights are subject to restrictions deemed necessary by Parliament to safeguard security, public order or morality.
He said freedom of expression in this country is guaranteed, however, the Constitution itself permits and anticipates the need for such restrictions in managing a multi-ethnic society.
"In this regard, the review of this act has taken into account the right to freedom of expression, including freedom of information, without compromising national security and public order, which must be safeguarded at the same time.
"The ministry ensures that amendment of this Act are aligned with 'necessary and appropriate' criteria under the Federal Constitution, without compromising national security, public order and morality," he said.
Saifuddin said the government will ensure that freedom of expression under the Federal Constitution, as well as citizens' right to access information, are upheld.
However, he said when such freedoms are used to undermine peace or incite hatred, it is the government's responsibility, mandated by the people, to regulate them through lawful and constitutional means.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Online scam losses surge to RM2.97b in 2025, says Home Ministry as fraud trend worsens across Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — The value of losses due to online fraud crimes at the national level has jumped drastically from RM1.57 billion in 2024 to RM2.97 billion in 2025 and reached RM830 million for the first five months of this year.
The Home Ministry (KDN) said according to the comparison of various modus operandi, non-existent investments consistently recorded the largest losses with a record of RM848.62 million in 2024, jumping to RM1.46 billion in 2025 and RM361.63 million this year.
According to KDN, the trend was closely followed by telecommunications crime which recorded RM497.12 million (2024), RM802.47 million (2025) and RM235.63 million (as of May 2026), while love scams continued to record losses of RM45.87 million (2024), RM47.44 million (2025) and RM17.76 million for the early period of this year.
From a geographical aspect, he said Selangor and Kuala Lumpur continued to record the highest losses, with Selangor jumping from RM446.16 million (2024) to RM986.79 million (2025), while Kuala Lumpur increased from RM293.30 million (2024) to RM782.86 million (2025).
“States with other economic focus such as Johor, Penang, and Perak also recorded significant year-on-year increases between 2024 and 2025 while Sabah and Sarawak also exhibited a similar trend of losses and increased to more than RM110 million in 2025,” said KDN in a written reply posted on the Parliament portal today.
The ministry was responding to a question from Datuk Seri Dr Ronald Kiandee (PN-Warisan) on the trend of losses due to online fraud crimes in the past three years by case and state categories, the success rate of recovery of victims’ money, as well as measures taken to improve the effectiveness of detection, freezing and returning funds to victims.
The Ministry of Home Affairs stressed its commitment to combating online crimes by strengthening the operation of the National Scam Response Centre (NSRC) which operates 24 hours a day by taking swift action through freezing bank accounts and transaction restrictions.
Since the establishment of NSRC in 2022, a total of RM32.49 million in fraud victim funds has been successfully seized and a total of RM10.9 million was returned to the victims.
“Based on statistics from 2022 to 2025, the seized money amounted to RM25.2 million with 29 per cent or RM7.3 million of which was successfully returned to the victims, while for the period January to May 2026, a total of RM7.25 million in funds was seized with RM3.57 million or 49 per cent returned to the victims.
“The increase in the percentage of funds returned proves that the function of NSRC as a national scam response centre is becoming more effective and is increasingly trusted by the people,” according to the ministry. — Bernama

Title: MRCB secures consent judgment in Shah Alam Stadium suit; case against blogger to continue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Malaysian Resources Corporation Bhd (MRCB) has secured a consent judgment against one of the defendants in its lawsuit alleging a conspiracy to cause economic damage through online posts about the demolition and redevelopment of the Shah Alam Stadium.
According to a statement, the consent judgment was entered at the Shah Alam High Court on June 22 against self-described activist Abdul Razak Ismail, one of two defendants named in the suit.
MRCB had sued Abdul Razak and blogger Murray Hunter over a series of posts published on Facebook and Hunter’s Substack platform that questioned the stadium demolition and redevelopment project.
The company alleged that the online publications amounted to economic sabotage and caused damage to MRCB.
The settlement was reached after Abdul Razak was cross-examined by MRCB’s counsel, Datuk Selva Mookiah, following which proceedings were paused and the defendant agreed to a consent judgment.
Under the terms recorded before Judicial Commissioner Shoba Rajah, Abdul Razak agreed to a permanent injunction restraining him from publishing or republishing the allegations that formed the subject of the suit.
He also undertook to remove all relevant postings within 72 hours, refrain from repeating the allegations, and not conspire with Hunter or any other parties in relation to publishing similar allegations concerning the matter.
Speaking to reporters after the proceedings, Selva said MRCB’s action against Hunter would continue.
Hunter, who Selva said is believed to be in Thailand based on media reports, is being sued in absentia and did not participate in the proceedings.
The court has fixed July 10 for submissions by the plaintiff in relation to its claim against Hunter, with judgment scheduled for July 17. Hearing dates previously set for June 23 and June 24 have been vacated.
Abdul Razak was represented by lawyer Rajesh Nagarajan.
MRCB noted that this was not the first legal dispute involving Abdul Razak over the Shah Alam Stadium project.
In 2023, he apologised to the company and agreed to retract remarks made in a press statement and on social media as part of a consent judgment in a defamation suit brought by MRCB over the same project.

Title: Stats Dept: Domestic tourism spending climbs 15.8pc to RM34b in Q1 2026 amid festive travel boost
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, June 24 — Domestic tourism expenditure recorded a double-digit growth of 15.8 per cent year-on-year to RM34 billion in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), according to the Bulletin of Malaysia Domestic Tourism Survey - First Quarter 2026 released by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today.
In a statement, DOSM said Malaysia’s domestic tourism maintained its growth momentum in the first three months of 2026, with total domestic visitors reaching 74.7 million, reflecting a year-on-year increase of 7.2 per cent.
The performance was further supported by a 0.9 per cent quarter-on-quarter growth compared to the fourth quarter of 2025, indicating continued stability in domestic travel demand.
“The growth was driven by festive and holiday travel during the quarter, particularly during Chinese New Year and Aidilfitri, which typically stimulate higher domestic travel activity, including visits to relatives and family holidays.
“These festive periods supported stronger domestic travel demand, contributing to higher visitor movements and broader tourism activity across the country,” the statement said.
Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin commented that the RM34 billion in domestic tourism expenditure recorded in the first quarter of 2026 reflected not only higher travel activity but also an increase in average spending per visitor.
“On a quarter-on-quarter basis, domestic tourism expenditure rose by 4.5 per cent, signalling continued resilience in tourism-related economic activities,” he was quoted as saying.
According to DOSM, the positive performance was underpinned by growth in key high-frequency indicators, with highway traffic increasing by 1.0 per cent year-on-year, retail fuel sales rising by 8.0 per cent, and domestic air passenger arrivals expanding by 9.6 per cent, reflecting stronger domestic mobility and travel demand.
The accommodation sector also demonstrated positive performance, with revenue increasing by 15.4 per cent, in line with rising tourism demand.
Occupancy rates for four-star and three-star hotels increased to 62.9 per cent (1Q 2025: 62.4 per cent) and 70.4 per cent (1Q 2025: 69.3 per cent), respectively, indicating a growing preference for mid-range accommodation options among travellers. — Bernama

Title: Major resurfacing works for pothole-hit Jalan Lahat to start next month
Article:
IPOH, June 24 — Road users can expect smoother, safer journeys soon as a major resurfacing and pothole repair project is set to begin along the heavily trafficked Jalan Lahat here.
Menglembu assemblyman Steven Chaw said the RM2.6 million project, funded through the Malaysian Road Records Information System (MARRIS) allocation, will cover approximately 1.9km in each direction, bringing the total resurfacing length to nearly 4km.
He said the approved works will cover the stretch from the Falim traffic lights to the flyover area, and onwards to the traffic lights near the Jalan Leong Boon Swee junction in Little India.
“Jalan Lahat is a very important route connecting three state constituencies, namely Buntong, Tebing Tinggi and Menglembu. Thousands of motorists travelling to and from Ipoh use this road every day.
“This is good news not only for residents in the three constituencies, but also for the many road users who rely on Jalan Lahat daily,” he told a press conference at Jalan Lahat today.
Chaw said the project is scheduled to begin in July and is expected to be completed within three weeks, by August.
He said Jalan Lahat stretches about 10km to 11km, but the current project will focus on the most critical section, where road conditions have deteriorated significantly.
Chaw said concerns over the road’s condition have recently gained traction on social media, with members of the public highlighting potholes and damaged road surfaces along the route.
“One individual recently stopped his vehicle on the Jalan Lahat flyover to record a video showing a large pothole and posted it on social media to warn motorists,” he said.
He also revealed that his service centre had received reports of 20 vehicles believed to have suffered tyre punctures this month after hitting potholes along the road, prompting affected motorists to seek advice on compensation claims.
However, he noted that no major or fatal accidents had been reported so far.
Meanwhile, Ipoh city councillor Sivam Kalimuthu said the road serves schools, commercial areas and pedestrian zones, making it one of the busiest stretches in the city.
He said temporary patching works had previously been carried out using local allocations, but the repairs proved ineffective due to constant exposure to rain, hot weather and heavy vehicle traffic.
